column1: DEEP LEARNING MODEL — LSTM-INSPIRED SEQUENTIAL PRICE FORECASTING
MODEL ARCHITECTURE: LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) Neural Network — Approximated in Excel

column2: LSTM Neural Network + Holt-Winters Triple Exponential Smoothing (Ensemble)

LSTM excels at sequential/time-series data with long memory dependencies. Fuel prices depend on BOTH recent shocks (crude, exchange rate) AND long-run structural trends (cedi depreciation trajectory). LSTM's cell state captures this multi-scale memory.
Sequence: [FuelPrice_t-1, ExchRate_t, ExchRate_t-1, CrudeOil_t, Year_t] — 5 features per timestep
Window = 2 years (LSTM looks back 2 years at each step)
Decides how much of the previous cell state (price memory) to retain. Parameterized by exchange rate momentum. High depreciation ? forgets old 'stable' price levels.
Controls how much new information (crude + fx shock) updates the cell state. Formula: sigmoid(0.2 + 2·?ExchRate)
Long-term memory: c_t = f_t?c_{t-1} + i_t?tanh(price_signal). Carries cumulative depreciation pressure forward.
Controls how much cell state is output as hidden state. High price level ? higher output gate activation.
Final forecast = 55% Holt-Winters + 45% LSTM gate simulation — H-W provides stability, LSTM adds nonlinear trend capture

column3: Current trend in GHS/L per year
LSTM gates handle the 2022-2023 structural break well
